originalTitle: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
title: World's oldest leader to get a deputy for first time in 43-year rule
content: Cameroon's President Paul Biya, 93, won his eighth successive term last year in a disputed election.

originalTitle: Senegal bans ministers from foreign travel as oil price rise bites
title: Senegal's Smart Move: Ministers Stay Home to Save Money!
content:
Hello, Friends from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you big news from a special country in Africa called Senegal. Senegal is a beautiful place with warm people and sunny beaches. Today, we have an important story about how Senegal is making smart choices with its money.

Important People Staying Home

Imagine your dad or mom, who works very hard. In a country, there are many important people who work hard to help everyone. We call these people "ministers." Ministers are like super-helpers for the whole country! They make sure schools are good, hospitals have doctors, and roads are smooth.

What is "Foreign Travel"?

Sometimes, these ministers need to fly on airplanes to other countries far away. This is called "foreign travel." They might go to visit other leaders to talk about important things, like how to make trade better or how to help each other. It's like going on a field trip, but for grown-ups who run a country!

No More Faraway Trips for Now!

But guess what? The President of Senegal has asked all his ministers to stop their foreign travel for a little while. That means no more airplane trips to other countries for these important people. They will stay right at home in Senegal.

Why Are They Staying Home?

Why would they do this? It's all about something very important: **money**. Every country has money to spend on things like schools, hospitals, and keeping the country safe. The government needs to be very careful with this money.

What is Fuel?

The reason they are saving money is because of something called "fuel." What is fuel? Well, it's the special stuff that makes cars go "vroom," buses roll down the street, and airplanes fly high in the sky! It's also used to make electricity for our homes and lights. Things like petrol, diesel, and gas are all types of fuel. **Did you know?** Fuel comes from deep inside the Earth! It's called oil, and it's like a special energy juice for our world!

Why is Fuel So Important?

Fuel is super important for everything we do. Think about it: * School buses use fuel to take you to school. * Trucks use fuel to bring yummy food to your local shop. * Ambulances use fuel to rush people to the hospital. * Your parents' car uses fuel to take you on fun trips! * Airplanes use fuel to fly people and goods across the world. So, fuel helps our country move and grow.

Fuel is Getting Expensive!

Lately, the price of fuel has become very, very high. Imagine you usually buy a small sweet for 5 coins. But suddenly, the shop owner says it now costs 10 coins! That's how it is with fuel right now. It's costing almost *double* what the government thought it would cost. **Did you know?** When fuel gets expensive, it can make other things more expensive too, like food, because trucks need fuel to bring the food to us!

What is a Budget?

Before a new year starts, the government makes a plan for all its money. This plan is called a "budget." It's like your mom or dad planning how much money they will spend on food, clothes, and school fees for the month. They try to guess how much everything will cost.

Senegal's Money Plan is Stretched

The government of Senegal made a budget. They planned to spend a certain amount of money on fuel. But now, fuel is so much more expensive! It's like they planned to buy one sweet, but now it costs enough money to buy two sweets! This means their money plan, or "finances," are "stretched." It means they don't have as much money for other things because fuel is eating up so much.

How Staying Home Helps

By telling ministers to stop foreign travel, Senegal's government is saving money. How? Because airplane tickets cost a lot! And staying in other countries costs money too. So, by staying home, they are being smart and saving those important coins. This saved money can then be used for other important things. Maybe for new books for schools, or medicine for hospitals, or fixing roads. It helps the country keep its promises to its people.

Why This Matters to You

You might think, "I'm just a kid, why does this news matter to me?" Well, it matters a lot! When the government saves money, it means there's more money to make your country a better place. It means more resources for your schools, better healthcare for your family, and safer communities. It shows that even big countries like Senegal need to be careful with money, just like you might save your pocket money for something special.

What Can We Do?

Even as kids, we can help! * We can try not to waste electricity at home. * We can walk or cycle more instead of always using a car, if it's safe. * We can remember that using things wisely helps everyone. Every little bit helps our wonderful continent, Africa, and countries like Senegal, grow strong and bright!

Senegal's Smart Choice!

So, Senegal is making a smart choice by having its ministers stay home. It's a way to save money because fuel is very expensive right now. This helps make sure there's enough money for all the important things the country needs, like schools, hospitals, and roads. It shows that being careful and wise with money is always a good idea, for big governments and for us too! Senegal is working hard to be strong and ready for the future.
summary: Senegal's government is saving money by asking its important helpers (ministers) to stop flying to other countries because fuel (like petrol for cars and planes) has become very expensive, making it harder to pay for other important things for the country.
whyMatters: This is important because when the government saves money, it can use it for schools, hospitals, and making the country better for everyone, including kids like you. It shows how being careful with money helps a whole country.

originalTitle: 'We want a voice in our land' - the people evicted to build Nigeria's capital
title: Homes and Promises in Nigeria!
content:
Jambo, wonderful friends! It's your friendly African news reporter here, ready to share a very important story from our big continent. Today, we're going to Nigeria, a beautiful country with lots of amazing people.

What is a Capital City?

Every country has a special city. This city is called the 'capital city'. It's like the heart of the country! Big meetings happen there. Important people work there. Nigeria has a capital city called Abuja. Abuja is a very important city. It is where the country's leaders meet. They make big decisions there. Decisions that help everyone in Nigeria. Did you know? Abuja was built many years ago! Before Abuja, another city called Lagos was the capital.

Building a Big New City

A long, long time ago, the leaders of Nigeria wanted to build a brand new capital city. They wanted it to be shiny and new. They chose a special place for Abuja. But guess what? People already lived in that special place! Families had their homes there. Farmers grew food there. Children played there. These people loved their homes. They loved their land. It was where their families had lived for many, many years.

Saying Goodbye to Their Homes

The government asked these people to move. They said, 'Please move, so we can build this big new city for everyone in Nigeria.' It must have been very hard for these families to leave their homes. Imagine leaving your comfy bed. Imagine leaving your garden. Imagine leaving your friends. It would make you feel very sad, wouldn't it? The people were sad, but they understood. They wanted to help their country grow. So, they agreed to move.

The Big Promises

When the people agreed to move, the government made some big promises. A promise is like saying, 'I will surely do this for you!' What kind of promises? The government said they would give the people new homes. Homes that were just as good, or even better! They promised to give them new land for farming. They also promised to help the children go to new schools. And help the families get new jobs. These promises made the people feel a little better about moving. Did you know? Keeping promises is very important. It shows you care about people!

What Happened Next?

Many years have passed since then. Abuja is now a very big and beautiful city. It has tall buildings and busy roads. It is a proud capital city. But what about the people who moved? The people who gave up their homes so Abuja could be built? This is where the story gets a little sad. These people say that many of the promises were not kept. The government did not give them new homes. Or the homes were not good enough. They did not get new land to farm. They did not get help for schools. They did not get new jobs easily.

Feeling Sad and Forgotten

The people feel sad. They feel left out. They feel like their voices are not being heard. They helped their country, but now they need help too. They just want what was promised to them. They want to be able to live happily. They want safe homes. They want their children to go to school. They want to feel important, just like everyone else. They want their leaders to remember them. Did you know? Everyone has a right to a safe home and a good education!

What Can We Learn?

This story teaches us something very important. It teaches us about being fair. And it teaches us about keeping our promises. When someone makes a promise, they should try very hard to keep it. Especially when it's about helping people live happy lives. The people who moved for Abuja want their leaders to listen. They want to say, 'Please remember us! Please help us get what you promised.' They want a voice. A voice means they want to be heard. They want to tell their story and ask for help.

A Hope for Tomorrow

We hope that the leaders in Nigeria will listen to these people. We hope they will find a way to help them. Every person deserves a good home. Every child deserves to learn. Every family deserves to be happy. Let's all hope for a happy ending for our friends in Nigeria. Let's hope their voices are heard loudly and clearly. Thank you for listening, wonderful friends. Until next time, keep being kind and curious! Did you know? Nigeria is home to over 200 million people, making it the most populated country in Africa!
summary: Families who moved from their homes so Nigeria could build its capital city, Abuja, are asking the government to keep its old promises.
whyMatters: It's important because everyone deserves a home, and leaders should always keep their promises to help their people live happily.
